mission.title: Install and Configure Composefs on Kubernetes
mission.description: Composefs provides a flexible mechanism to support read-only mountable filesystem trees, combining the reliability of disk images with the flexibility of files. Installing Composefs allows you to efficiently manage filesystem images while avoiding common drawbacks associated with traditional disk images.

steps.title: Add the Composefs Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Composefs Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add composefs https://charts.example.com/composefs
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Composefs using Helm
steps.description:
Install Composefs in a new namespace called 'composefs' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install composefs composefs/composefs --namespace composefs --create-namespace --version v1.0.8
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Composefs pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace composefs
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Composefs deployment to ensure it runs efficiently:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/composefs --namespace composefs --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Port forward to access Composefs
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Composefs service to access it:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/composefs --namespace composefs 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Composefs pods running in the 'composefs' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. Resource limits will be applied to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add composefs https://charts.example.com/composefs
helm repo update
helm install composefs composefs/composefs --namespace composefs --create-namespace --version v1.0.8

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Composefs Helm release from the 'composefs' namespace to remove the deployment and associated resources:
```bash
helm uninstall composefs --namespace composefs
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that may have been created during the installation:
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace composefs
kubectl delete crd composefs.*
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'composefs' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace composefs
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and any important data related to Composefs:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace composefs -o yaml > composefs-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Composefs release to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade composefs composefs/composefs --namespace composefs --version v1.0.9
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Composefs pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace composefs
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Composefs pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace composefs
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Composefs service, ensure that the service is running and check the port forwarding command:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace composefs
kubectl port-forward svc/composefs --namespace composefs 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting due to insufficient resources, check the resource limits and requests:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment composefs --namespace composefs
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the Helm release is not found, verify the release name and namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace composefs
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
